tasks.instruction: Add a new exported function readAuditReport() to pathService.js. The function must: (1) build the report path using path.join(getRepoRoot(), 'live_upload_audit', 'upload_comparison.json'); (2) if that path does not exist (use fs.existsSync), return the object { success: false, errorMessage: 'upload_comparison.json not found in live_upload_audit/' }; (3) otherwise read and parse the file with JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(path, 'utf8')); (4) return the object { success: true, generatedAt: data.generated_at_utc, offlineCount: data.offline_count, platforms: { youtube: { uploaded: data.platforms.youtube.uploaded_count, notUploaded: data.platforms.youtube.not_uploaded_count }, instagram: { uploaded: data.platforms.instagram.uploaded_count, notUploaded: data.platforms.instagram.not_uploaded_count }, facebook: { uploaded: data.platforms.facebook.uploaded_count, notUploaded: data.platforms.facebook.not_uploaded_count } } }; wrap the parse in try/catch and on error return { success: false, errorMessage: 'Failed to parse audit report: ' + error.message }. Add readAuditReport to the module.exports object alongside the existing exports.

Add a require for pathService at the top of the file: add the line 'const pathService = require("../services/pathService");' after the existing require statements. Then inside the registerSystemHandlers function, add a new IPC handler immediately after the existing 'save-workflow-settings' handle block. The new handler must be: ipcMain.handle('get-audit-report', async function handleGetAuditReport() { return pathService.readAuditReport(); }); Do not change any other handlers or exports.

In preload.js, inside the contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld('api', { ... }) object, add a new entry after getToolStatus. The new entry must be: getAuditReport: function getAuditReport() { return ipcRenderer.invoke('get-audit-report'); } Make sure there is a comma after the preceding getToolStatus entry. Do not change any other entries.

In Audit.jsx, modify the Audit function to add an audit report summary panel. Add a second useState at the top of the Audit function body: const [auditReport, setAuditReport] = useState(null); and const [reportLoading, setReportLoading] = useState(false);. Add an async function inside Audit called handleLoadReport: it sets reportLoading to true, calls const result = await window.api.getAuditReport(), sets auditReport to result if result.success is true (otherwise sets it to null and could log the error), then sets reportLoading to false. In the JSX, directly below the closing of the .page-heading div and above the .audit-table-shell div, insert a new block: a div with style={{ marginBottom: 24 }} containing: (1) a button labeled reportLoading ? 'Loading...' : 'Load Audit Report from Disk' with onClick={handleLoadReport} styled with padding '6px 16px', borderRadius 6, border 'none', background '#4f46e5', color '#fff', cursor 'pointer', fontSize 12; (2) if auditReport is not null, show a div below the button styled with background '#111827', borderRadius 8, padding 14, marginTop 12 displaying: a p with fontSize 12 and color '#6b7280' showing 'Report generated: ' + auditReport.generatedAt; followed by a p showing 'Offline videos: ' + auditReport.offlineCount; followed by a simple grid (display flex, gap 16) of three stat blocks each showing platform name, uploaded count, and not-uploaded count from auditReport.platforms.youtube, auditReport.platforms.instagram, auditReport.platforms.facebook. Do not change AuditAction, getRowClassName, formatStatus, or the existing table.

In useAppStore.js, make three changes. (1) Add commandHistory: [] to the initial state object, placed after the logEntries: [] line. (2) Add a new action addCommandToHistory immediately after the clearLogs action function: addCommandToHistory: function addCommandToHistory(entry) { set(function updateHistory(state) { return { commandHistory: state.commandHistory.concat(entry).slice(-20) }; }); }, (3) In the startConsole async function, after the existing set({ uploadStatus: response, errorMessage: response.errorMessage || '' }) call, add these two lines: const state2 = get(); get().addCommandToHistory({ id: Date.now(), timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), platforms: Object.keys(state2.uploadPlatforms).filter(function filterActive(p) { return state2.uploadPlatforms[p]; }), commandPreview: response.commandPreview || '' }); Do not change any other state, actions, or functions.

In Console.jsx, add a command history panel below the console-viewer div. First add a new selector at the top of the Console function (after the existing selectors): const commandHistory = useAppStore(function selectCommandHistory(state) { return state.commandHistory; }); and const runUpload = useAppStore(function selectRunUpload(state) { return state.runUpload; });. Then add a useState for history panel open state: const [historyOpen, setHistoryOpen] = useState(false);. After the closing of the console-viewer div, add a new section: a div with style={{ borderTop: '1px solid #1f2937', marginTop: 12, padding: '12px 16px' }} containing: (1) a button that toggles historyOpen, labeled historyOpen ? 'Hide Command History' : 'Show Command History (' + commandHistory.length + ')' styled similarly to the existing console buttons; (2) if historyOpen is true, show a div containing the last 10 commandHistory entries in reverse order (use .slice(-10).reverse()), each rendered as a div with style={{ background: '#111827', borderRadius: 6, padding: '8px 12px', marginTop: 8, fontSize: 12 }} showing: a span with color '#6b7280' for the timestamp formatted via formatTime, a span with color '#e2e8f0' for the platforms joined by ', ', a p with color '#9ca3af' for the first 100 characters of commandPreview + '...' if longer, and a button labeled 'Run Again' with onClick={runUpload} styled with padding '3px 10px', borderRadius 4, border 'none', background '#4f46e5', color '#fff', cursor 'pointer', marginTop 4. Do not change the toolbar, log viewer, search input, or existing buttons.

In Dashboard.jsx, find the existing div with style containing display:'flex', gap:12, marginTop:20 that holds the three quick-launch buttons (Start Upload, View Library, Open Console). Add three more buttons inside that same div, after the existing 'Open Console' button: (1) a button with onClick that calls useAppStore.getState().setActivePage('audit'), label 'View Audit', styled identically to the existing buttons; (2) a button with onClick that calls useAppStore.getState().setActivePage('tools'), label 'Open Tools', styled identically; (3) a button with onClick that calls useAppStore.getState().setActivePage('setup'), label 'Run Setup', styled identically. Do not change any other parts of the component.

In Library.jsx, add a selected-video detail panel that appears below the library-grid div when a video is selected. First, add two new selectors inside the Library function after the existing selectVideo selector: const setActivePage = useAppStore(function selectSetActivePage(state) { return state.setActivePage; }); and const selectedVideo = useAppStore(function selectSelectedVideo(state) { return state.videoList.find(function findVid(v) { return v.id === state.selectedVideoId; }) || null; });. Then, directly after the closing of the library-grid div (and before the closing ), add a conditional block: if selectedVideo is not null, render a div with style={{ marginTop: 24, background: '#111827', borderRadius: 10, padding: 20 }} containing: (1) an h2 with fontSize 15, color '#e2e8f0', marginBottom 12 labeled 'Selected Video'; (2) a p with fontSize 13, color '#e2e8f0' showing selectedVideo.title; (3) a p with fontSize 12, color '#6b7280' showing selectedVideo.relativePath || 'No path'; (4) a p with fontSize 12, color '#6b7280' showing 'Duration: ' + (selectedVideo.duration || 'Unknown'); (5) a div showing platform flags using three span elements for YT, IG, FB each colored green (#4ade80) if true or gray (#6b7280) if false, with text 'YT: Yes/No', 'IG: Yes/No', 'FB: Yes/No', spaced with gap 12 in a flex div; (6) a button labeled 'Edit Metadata' with onClick that calls setActivePage('metadata'), styled with padding '7px 16px', borderRadius 6, border 'none', background '#4f46e5', color '#fff', cursor 'pointer', fontSize 13, marginTop 12. Do not change the toolbar, search, sort, platform filter, or the library-grid rendering.
